title: Protect and mask sensitive CI/CD variables
description: Mark secret variables protected so only protected branches/tags see them, and masked so they are scrubbed from logs.
body: Use when adding a credential or token as a CI/CD variable, or reviewing variable settings. Do not store a secret as an unprotected, unmasked variable, because unprotected variables are exposed to pipelines on any branch (including attacker MR branches) and unmasked values print in job logs. Actions: set the variable Protected so it is available only to protected branches/tags, set it Masked so GitLab scrubs it from logs, and scope it to the right environment. Validation signals include the secret being unavailable on a feature-branch pipeline and absent from job log output. Risk boundary: exposing a protected variable to an unprotected branch or a fork MR is a secret-exposure finding requiring a human gate.
